scenario: Aurora Bank, a retail bank serving 80000 customers, evaluates Camunda 8.8 out-of-the-box connectors to add 3 third-party integrations to its loan approval process in Web Modeler. A solution architect must explain how a connector is technically structured and where it is configured before the modeling team begins drag-and-drop work.
question: Which statement correctly describes how a Camunda 8.8 out-of-the-box connector is structured and configured?

options.text: A connector consists of business logic implemented as a job worker plus a modeling UI provided by an element template, and is configured through the properties panel in Web Modeler
A connector is a single Zeebe broker plugin compiled into the cluster jar that declares its modeling fields in a YAML file embedded inside each BPMN diagram at deployment time
A connector is a Camunda 7 external task client packaged with each diagram and is configured by editing BPMN XML attributes via a command-line tool prior to deployment
A connector is a Zeebe gateway interceptor that runs inside the cluster gateway pod and is configured exclusively through environment variables set on that pod at startup time

a.text: Correct. The docs state that connectors consist of two parts: the business logic is implemented as a job worker and the user interface during modeling is provided using an element template, and they are configured via the properties panel on the right side of Web Modeler.

b.text: Incorrect. The runtime side of a connector is a job worker, not a broker plugin, and the modeling UI is supplied by an element template, not by YAML embedded inside the BPMN diagram.

c.text: Incorrect. Out-of-the-box connectors are not Camunda 7 external task clients; they are added inside Web Modeler and configured via the properties panel rather than by editing BPMN XML from the command line.

d.text: Incorrect. Connectors run as job workers behind an element template and are configured through the modeler properties panel; they are not gateway interceptors driven only by gateway pod environment variables.

explanation: An out-of-the-box connector is composed of a job worker for the runtime business logic and an element template that drives the modeling UI. The user fills out the required fields in the properties panel on the right side of the Web Modeler screen.
